actionable_feedback: Open inventory_stress_simulation.xlsx in Excel (or an environment with openpyxl) and verify that formula cells are present for all computed outputs (safetyStock, daily flows, projections, unmet demand, scenario calculations) and not pre-cached single-cell values.
If any cells are cached values, replace them with live formulas referencing input cells or intermediate named ranges so reviewers and automated tools can trace calculations.
Add an explicit 'Inputs traceable = TRUE' checklist cell and a short 'traceability notes' area listing the authoritative input cells/rows so automated validators can easily find inputs.

Add a simple per-day mass-balance validation table (plant outflow = sum(DC inflows) per day) to demonstrate conservation and make it easy for reviewers to confirm allocation logic without running traces.

On the mitigation/decision sheet, include explicit references to the exact trigger cells used for recommendations and a before/after numeric column showing the expected benefit (e.g., reduction in unmet demand or stockout days) for each recommendation.

Add at least two dedicated recalculation scenario tabs (fully wired formulas) that reproduce the sensitivity cases listed in scenarioNotes so reviewers can toggle scenarios and see recalculated outputs.
After making the workbook changes, re-run the verifier/grader so the artifact can be parsed and the earlier parsing limitation can be cleared.
artifact_specific_comments: Builder metadata and trace indicate 'cached formulas' were used when generating the workbook. This is acceptable for reproducible numeric outputs but prevents formula-level verification by automated parsers.
The artifact file path and content hash are present in evidence; manual inspection recommended to confirm formula visibility.
feedback_summary: Baseline attempt passed all rubric checks and received a final score of 1.0. Grader and verifier agree numeric outputs (safety stocks, day14/day21 inventories, plantEnd, unmet demand, scenarioNotes) are consistent with the simulation described in the trace. The only outstanding issue is that the environment could not parse the delivered XLSX (openpyxl_unavailable) and the workbook builder metadata indicates cached formulas were used; this prevents automated verification that formula cells are visible (not cached values). A manual workbook inspection or a rebuild removing cached single-cell values is recommended before final publication.

known_limitations: Artifact preview parsing failed in grader/verifier environment (openpyxl_unavailable).
Grader's semantic assertions about formula visibility cannot be fully confirmed without opening the workbook.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: One issue: artifact preview parsing unavailable (openpyxl_unavailable). Manual workbook formula verification required.
